## /docs/getting-started/editor-basics

---
title: Editor Basics
description: The editor layout, toolbar, keyboard shortcuts, file upload, and the Examples dropdown — everything you need to work efficiently in Glyphic.
---

# Editor Basics

## Layout

The editor is a two-column layout: source on the left, live preview on the right. The toolbar runs across the top.

## Keyboard shortcuts

| Shortcut | Action |
|---|---|
| `⌘S` / `Ctrl+S` | Save (no-op in browser, triggers export prompt in app) |
| `⌘K` | Open search |
| `⌘Z` | Undo |
| `⌘⇧Z` | Redo |
| `⌘+` | Zoom in |
| `⌘-` | Zoom out |
| `⌘0` | Reset zoom |

## File upload

Drag a `.mmd` or `.txt` file onto the editor to load it. Click the upload area to browse.

## The toolbar

```
[Logo] [Examples ▼] [Share] [Export ▼] [Light/Dark] [Grid] [Prettify]
```

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam.

## /docs/getting-started

---
title: Getting Started
description: Everything you need to start using Glyphic — from installation to your first animated diagram.
---

# Getting Started

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Glyphic runs entirely in the browser for the core editor experience, with an optional backend for sharing and server-side rendering.

## Prerequisites

| Requirement | Version |
|---|---|
| Modern browser | Chrome 110+, Firefox 115+, Safari 16+ |
| Node.js (for self-hosting) | 18.x or later |
| pnpm (for self-hosting) | 8.x or later |

## What's in this section

- **Installation** — add Glyphic to your workflow or self-host it
- **Quickstart** — render your first diagram in under two minutes
- **Your First Project** — a complete walkthrough from blank canvas to shared link

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua.


---

## /docs/getting-started/quickstart

---
title: Quickstart
description: Render your first Mermaid diagram in Glyphic in under two minutes.
---

# Quickstart

## Step 1: Open the editor

Navigate to [glyphic.cc/app](https://glyphic.cc/app). The editor opens with an example diagram pre-loaded.

## Step 2: Write a diagram

Clear the editor and paste:

```mermaid
flowchart TD
  A[User] --> B[Load Balancer]
  B --> C[API Server]
  C --> D[(Database)]
```

The preview panel on the right updates instantly.

## Step 3: Add an animation

Append this block below your diagram:

```
@animate
  A -> B: duration=1s delay=0
  B -> C: duration=1s delay=0.5s
  C -> D: duration=1s delay=1s
@end
```

Click the play button in the preview panel. Edges animate in sequence.

## Step 4: Export

Click **Export** in the toolbar and choose **GIF** to download an animated export.

| Export format | Use case |
|---|---|
| SVG | Scalable, embeddable in docs |
| PNG | Slide decks, PR comments |
| GIF | Animated, universal support |
| WebM | High-quality video, Notion embeds |

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit.

## /docs

---
title: Welcome to Glyphic
description: Glyphic is a visual editor for Mermaid diagrams. Build, animate, and share architecture diagrams without leaving your browser.
---

# Welcome to Glyphic

Glyphic turns plain-text Mermaid diagrams into living, interactive visuals. Whether you're documenting a microservices architecture, walking through a deploy pipeline, or presenting a system design in a team meeting, Glyphic gives you the tools to make diagrams that move, compare, and tell stories.

## What you can do with Glyphic

- **Edit** Mermaid source in a live editor with instant preview
- **Animate** edges and nodes with the `@animate` meta-block
- **Compare** before/after states with `@compare` split view
- **Export** diagrams as SVG, PNG, GIF, or WebM
- **Share** a view-only link so teammates see the diagram without touching the source

## How this documentation is organized

| Section | What you'll find |
|---|---|
| Getting Started | Install, first project, quickstart guide |
| Guides | Core concepts, editor workflow, animations, deployment |
| API Reference | Authentication, Editor API, Rendering API, Webhooks |

## Quick links

- [Installation](/docs/getting-started/installation)
- [Quickstart](/docs/getting-started/quickstart)
- [Core Concepts](/docs/guides/core-concepts)
- [Editor API](/docs/api-reference/editor-api)

title: "@animate"
description: Animate edges and nodes in your Mermaid diagram with flowing particles, pulsing borders, and status indicators.

---
title: "@compare"
description: Show two diagram states side-by-side with before/after split view and optional diff highlighting.

title: Meta-blocks
description: Glyphic extends Mermaid with meta-blocks — structured directives that add animation, status overlays, presentation labels, and split-view comparison without changing your Mermaid source.

## /docs/meta-blocks/present

---
title: "@present"
description: Add presentation overlays to your diagram — badges attached to nodes and fixed text overlays positioned on the canvas.
---

# @present

Coming soon.


---

## /docs/meta-blocks/status

---
title: "@status"
description: Turn your diagram into a live NOC board by mapping nodes to health states — healthy, degraded, or down.
